body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Keep deployment-value clients from overriding provider keys**

`newChatTestOptions` now always injects `WithFakeOpenAICompatProvider` when `ChatProviderAPIKeys` is nil, and `newChatClientWithDeploymentValues` routes through this helper. That means tests that intentionally set deployment keys (for example `values.AI.BridgeConfig.LegacyOpenAI.Key`) no longer exercise deployment-derived key behavior, because `coderd.New` will consume the injected `ChatProviderAPIKeys` override instead. In practice, deployment-key test cases can pass for the wrong reason, which weakens regression coverage for deployment-backed provider policy paths.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
